title: carduus pycnocephalus subsp. albidus
intro: Carduus pycnocephalus subsp. albidus, also known as the biennial thistle, is a perennial plant species with a white or pale yellow flower head. The biennial thistle has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Carduus pycnocephalus subsp. albidus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopausal disorders, such as hot flashes, and its ability to act as a digestive aid. Herbal preparations of the biennial thistle include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support women's health and promote digestive well-being.
